fault_hypothesis: The function never normalises the adjusted list before returning it; line 68 returns the raw blended values instead of dividing each element by the total sum, so inputs that don't already sum to 1.0 produce un-normalised output.

constraints.intent: The returned list must sum to 1.0 (normalised), which fails when inputs do not already sum to 1.0.
Each appended blended value should be non-negative after clamping.
Right after initialisation, adjusted must be empty before the loop fills it.
The returned allocation vector must always be normalised to sum to 1.0.
